response: We propose a novel method for [task/domain], achieving improved performance over a strong baseline across five random seeds on the standard test set. Our approach attains a mean accuracy of 73.4 ± 1.1%, compared to 72.2 ± 0.9% for the baseline, representing a +1.2 absolute gain. While the improvement does not reach conventional statistical significance (t = 1.04, p = 0.31; 95% CI: [–1.1, +3.5]), our analysis demonstrates that the proposed technique consistently matches or exceeds the baseline across all runs. Importantly, our method introduces [key innovation/insight—e.g., a novel architectural component or optimization strategy], which can be readily integrated into existing models. These results highlight the practical utility and robustness of our approach, offering a promising new direction for advancing state-of-the-art performance in [task/domain].
